flags.note: Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
αἰώνιος — "eternal" (timeless) vs. "age-long" (pertaining to the coming age). Some argue it describes quality of life in the Age to Come, not mere duration.
πνεῦμα — "spirit" vs. "Spirit." Capitalization is a theological decision, not a grammatical fact. Greek has no capital letters.
